principles.lead: When delivery stalls, I don't ask who is failing.
principles.rest: I ask what system structure would make capable people produce exactly these results.

principles.lead: Predictability doesn't come from tighter control.
principles.rest: It comes from making the system observable.

principles.lead: Most organizations don't fail because they chose the wrong framework.
principles.rest: They fail because they never identify the real problem the framework is supposed to solve.

principles.lead: Sustainable transformation happens when improvement becomes part of the system,
principles.rest: not when coaches become part of the system.

principles.lead: The larger the organization, the less important authority becomes.
principles.rest: And the more important alignment becomes.

principles.lead: Every organization reaches a point where yesterday's strengths become tomorrow's constraints.
principles.rest: Frameworks don't solve that. Leadership does.
